fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer pharmacist license to vermont, pharmacist compact vermont, vermont pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Vermont Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: Office of Professional Regulation, 89 Main Street, 3rd Floor, Montpelier, VT 05620-3402

quick_facts.processing_time: Varies, typically 4-8 weeks once all documentation is complete

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Vermont.

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Visit the Vermont OPR's Online Services portal (MyLicense Vermont) and create a new user account. Once registered and logged in, select 'Apply for a new license/certification' and choose the appropriate 'Pharmacist (by Exam)' or 'Pharmacist (by Endorsement)' application.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Complete the detailed online application form within the MyLicense Vermont system and pay the required application fee of $100 using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: All applicants are required to complete a fingerprint-supported criminal background check through IdentoGO. Follow the specific instructions and scheduling process provided on the OPR website for Pharmacists.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Documents
steps.description: Ensure your official pharmacy school transcripts are sent directly from your institution to the Vermont OPR. If applying by exam, arrange for your NAPLEX and MPJE scores to be sent directly from NABP to the Vermont OPR. For endorsement, complete the NABP e-Profile ID and verification request.

steps.title: Submit Additional Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload any other required supporting documents as outlined in your application checklist through the MyLicense Vermont portal, or arrange for their direct submission to the OPR as specified.

steps.title: Application Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Vermont Office of Professional Regulation will review your complete application and all submitted materials. Once all requirements are met and verified, your Vermont Pharmacist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Vermont. To work in Vermont, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.processing_time: Varies, typically 4-8 weeks once all documentation is complete
reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Vermont
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Vermont, navigate to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation's Online Services portal, often referred to as MyLicense Vermont, where you can submit all required application materials.

official_links.portal_name: Vermont Office of Professional Regulation's Online Services portal (MyLicense Vermont)
official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Online Services portal (MyLicense Vermont), click 'Register a New Account', complete the registration form with your personal details, and create a username and password. Once registered, you can log in to start your application.
